content.text:
All 21 tests pass. I wrote `test/merge.test.js` covering every documented behavior, and removed the vacuous starter test (`example.test.js`), which only asserted the return was an array.

Each test names the concrete bug it catches and asserts hand-computed values through the public interface. Coverage:

- **Empty / single** — `[] -> []`, single interval returned unchanged.
- **Overlap & containment** — `[1,4],[3,6] -> [1,6]`; fully-contained `[3,4]` inside `[1,10]` (guards against using `end` instead of `Math.max`).
- **Touch boundary (the key off-by-one)** — touching `[1,2],[2,3] -> [1,3]` merges; gap-of-1 `[1,2],[3,4]` stays separate. Tests both sides of the `start <= last.end` condition.
- **Sorting** — unsorted input is sorted; out-of-order overlaps still merge.
- **Chaining** — running-merge extension (`[1,3],[2,5],[4,8] -> [1,8]`) and end-max propagation (`[1,5],[2,10],[10,12] -> [1,12]`).
- **Duplicates & zero-length** — identical pairs collapse; `[n,n]` degenerate intervals both standalone and touching.
- **Negatives** — comparison across zero.
- **Errors** — `RangeError` when `start > end`, including when a *later* pair is the invalid one; and confirms `start == end` is valid (guards against an over-strict `>=`).
- **Immutability** — input array, input pairs, and returned references are all verified non-aliased.

result:
All 21 tests pass. I wrote `test/merge.test.js` covering every documented behavior, and removed the vacuous starter test (`example.test.js`), which only asserted the return was an array.

Each test names the concrete bug it catches and asserts hand-computed values through the public interface. Coverage:

- **Empty / single** — `[] -> []`, single interval returned unchanged.
- **Overlap & containment** — `[1,4],[3,6] -> [1,6]`; fully-contained `[3,4]` inside `[1,10]` (guards against using `end` instead of `Math.max`).
- **Touch boundary (the key off-by-one)** — touching `[1,2],[2,3] -> [1,3]` merges; gap-of-1 `[1,2],[3,4]` stays separate. Tests both sides of the `start <= last.end` condition.
- **Sorting** — unsorted input is sorted; out-of-order overlaps still merge.
- **Chaining** — running-merge extension (`[1,3],[2,5],[4,8] -> [1,8]`) and end-max propagation (`[1,5],[2,10],[10,12] -> [1,12]`).
- **Duplicates & zero-length** — identical pairs collapse; `[n,n]` degenerate intervals both standalone and touching.
- **Negatives** — comparison across zero.
- **Errors** — `RangeError` when `start > end`, including when a *later* pair is the invalid one; and confirms `start == end` is valid (guards against an over-strict `>=`).
- **Immutability** — input array, input pairs, and returned references are all verified non-aliased.
